GOOG.reasoning: 1. MACRO REGIME: Risk-on with transitional caveats. S&P 500 at fresh all-time highs above 7,200 (April 2026 best month since 2020). Fed held at 3.5-3.75% on April 29 with 4 dissents (most since Oct 1992) — unusual internal fracture. Chair Powell exits May 15; Kevin Warsh succession introduces hawkish policy uncertainty. Iran peace talks removing the dominant energy/inflation left-tail risk (WTI -2%). Net regime: risk-on but not cleanly — Fed leadership transition and elevated inflation (IMF: 4.4% headline) make this transitional. Alphabet/Amazon Q1 AI cloud beats support the AI capex supercycle narrative. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: GOOG is a clear sector outperformer. On April 30, GOOG gapped up strongly (open 371.25 → close 381.94) on 44.5M volume vs 17.1M 20-day avg (2.6x relative volume). ROC: +13.1% (5d), +14.8% (10d), +33.2% (21d). All EMAs aligned in uptrend; price 35-36% above 200-day SMA. This is extreme outperformance — likely post-earnings gap. However, this also flags the trade is LATE in the sector rotation move, not early. 3. ASYMMETRIC R/R — FAILS MINIMUM BAR: Current price ~381.94. Upside: nearest Fib extension 1.272 at 412.85 = +8.1% potential. Downside: nearest volume-confirmed hourly support at 342.69 = -10.3% risk. R/R ratio = 0.79:1 — well below the 2:1 minimum threshold. Even targeting the daily Fib 1.618 extension at 451.29 = +18.2% vs -10.3% downside gives 1.77:1, still below minimum. The stop level that would invalidate relative strength is the hourly support at 342.69, making asymmetry structurally poor at current entry. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION FLAGS: RSI 7-day = 97.75, RSI 14-day = 86.83, RSI 21-day = 89.31 — historically extreme overbought levels. Price is above upper Bollinger Band (pct_b = 1.14). Z-score vs 50-period SMA = 3.29 (more than 3 standard deviations above mean). Daily ATR = 2.49% on a post-gap parabolic move creates meaningful gap-fill risk. LESSON FLAG: The recent NVDA trade (-$264.65) was stopped out entering at RSI 81-87 in a stretched macro environment — GOOG's RSI is MORE extreme. Same regime context applies; confidence dialed down accordingly. CONCLUSION: The macro regime supports risk-on longs in AI/cloud. GOOG is demonstrably the strongest name in tech. However, the risk/reward math fails the 2:1 minimum bar at current prices, the RSI is at historic extremes (97.75), and the move is likely post-earnings — meaning the catalyst is already priced. Neutral here; a pullback to the 342-358 zone (hourly Fib 61.8%-78.6% retracement) would create a 3:1+ setup worth entering.
